mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll growth indicates robust labor demand and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings higher and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months but the S&P 500 still rises, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.25% after a period of near-zero rates, it signals the start of a tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after a lift-off the S&P 500 rises more than 5% over the next 60 trading days, or the signal fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling housing starts indicates a consumption shift away from durable goods and a maturing cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as the economy loses momentum.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (RSAFS growth * HOUST decline) has a coefficient that is not statistically significant at the 10% level in a predictive regression, or if the signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of falling unemployment (UNRATE declining more than 0.5 percentage points over the prior six months), a further month-over-month decline of at least 0.1 percentage point signals continued labor market recovery and supports positive equity returns.
regime_hint: falling unemployment regime (UNRATE declining > 0.5pp over 6 months)
failure_condition: If in the falling unemployment regime, UNRATE declines but the S&P 500 produces negative returns in more than 60% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index indicates rising inflation that erodes real earnings and prompts defensive positioning, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If a positive CPI surprise is followed by a positive S&P return in more than 50% of the next 5-day windows, or the information coefficient is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate while industrial production stagnates, inventories are drawn down in anticipation of future production increases, a dynamic that signals strengthening economic momentum and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the interaction of RSAFS growth and INDPRO growth (RSAFS > 0 and INDPRO < 0) does not lead to positive returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the spread coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline of more than 0.5% in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and broad economic weakness, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after an INDPRO drop of more than 0.5% the S&P 500 produces positive returns in more than 40% of instances, or the average 60-day return is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with rising housing starts reflects broad-based economic momentum in both labor and housing markets, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: recovery phase (declining UNRATE and rising HOUST)
failure_condition: If the interaction of declining UNRATE (month-over-month change <= -0.1%) and rising HOUST (month-over-month change >= 1%) fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of instances, or the joint effect is not significant at the 5% level.
